jobDescription: ZIELONA GÓ RAWho are Meta Pack? No we are not a packaging company – that’s Tetra Pack! We are a tech company that works with a lot of the world’s biggest ecommerce players to integrate them with over 450 carriers around the world to make delivery easy. We are a multi-tenant Saa S platform. We give them the platform to help consumers decide their delivery preference and track the parcel’s progress whilst also providing the retailer with intelligent smart decisions about how to send the parcel – all underpinned with lots of data. We work with well-known global retailers and major brands such as ASOS, Adidas, Burberry, John Lewis, Boohoo, e Bay, and Zalando. In fact we work with so many retailers and carriers it’s highly likely that you’ve interacted with us at some point when ordering goods online! In August 2018, we were acquired by Fortune 100’s 2nd fastest growing company, stamps.com. We have super ambitious and exciting plans all centred around our tech. Meta Pack will play a role in shipping around 600 million parcels in 2018 and with the wider stamps.com family the number rises to 2.5bn parcels. Meta Pack has been growing at 40% year on year over the last 5 years and continues to grow at a rapid rate. Why would I want to be an Requirements Analyst at Meta Pack? The requirements analyst focuses on the initial analysis of the carrier's work, its services and systems on which it operates. He works closely with carriers, clients, solution, sales and development departments. Its task is to collect and verify the carrier's requirements and identify potential challenges related to projects such as: integration of the carrier and customer with the Meta Pack platform, innovations in existing carrier modules, maintenance of existing modules, other changes regarding technical cooperation with the carrier What would I be doing? Creating IT solutions with pre-defined requirements Identifying challenges related to the implementation of the project Preparation of system customer requirements based on product analysis Creation, analysis and verification of functional specifications Estimation of the resources which needed to meet functional and non-functional requirements in the field of courier integration Regular update of progress, reporting of risk and emerging challenges Providing support for development teams during the project implementation Solving problems in cooperation with the client, suppliers, colleagues Building and maintaining good relationships with clients and carriers Ensuring effective, clear and timely internal and external communication Active acquisition of business knowledge related to carriers, customers and their logistics processes Regular development of your competences (including language) Striving to constantly improve everyday activities to increase work efficiency Who are you? Able to interact with customers on a technical and non-technical basis using English Strong communicator with influencing skills Able to work on your own initiative with limited supervision Excellent time management and organisational acumen Pro-active thinker and problem solver Able to manage multiple clients and projects/tasks simultaneously Have the capability to manage client expectations Strong troubleshooter: You will be responsible for detecting potential issues and implementing solutions when issues threaten to delay the timeline or impact budget or business operation What are the perks? Max.30 days holiday (increasing based on length of service once you have been with us for 2 years) Work in a great atmosphere, with people who like what they do and are willing to share their knowledge Private medical healthcare Discounted gym membership Free language courses - English, German, Spanish Fresh fruit, snacks and drinks Relax room Hardware budget to let you get what you need Dynamic, open culture with lots of social activities
